---
title: "LRPLF11: Factors associated with the level of nonword reading (N), off-floor"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPLF11 is the **level-factors** model for nonword reading (`nonword`).

::: {.callout-important title="Off-floor likelihood + level-model crossover caveat"}

Nonword reading is heavily floored, so the level outcome is the **off-floor indicator** (score $> 0$) at each timepoint, modelled by a Bernoulli; the linear predictor is the **log-odds of being off the floor at that timepoint** — pooling children who were never at floor, came off it, and returned to it — not a graded level. Under the revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, revised 2026-07-10) measured skill parents are **deliberately excluded** as post-treatment mediators of the group×time effect; only the revised-DAG confounders speech (SP = `deapp_c`) and phonological memory (RW = `erbto`) — each with a missing-indicator — enter as adjusters. `group x time` is a per-timepoint off-floor log-odds for the group (`b_grp_time[t]`); after t2 the waitlist crosses over, so the **clean randomised contrast lives only at t2** (`b_grp_time[1]`, labelled *causal*); t3/t4 are post-crossover cohort/timing terms. The child random intercept is only a partial, shrunken stand-in for between-child heterogeneity and does **not** control latent general ability. `ability x time` and `group x ability` complete the focal set. SES excluded (non-DAG / redundant).

:::

## Model

$$
(N_t > 0) \sim \operatorname{Bernoulli}(\operatorname{expit}(\eta_t)),\quad
\eta_t = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{time}[t]} + b_{\text{grp}[t]}\text{group}
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std},t} + g_{\text{ability}[t]}z(\text{blocks})
  + \gamma_{\text{grp\_ability}}\,\text{group}\,z(\text{blocks}) + \sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c) + u_{\text{child}}.
$$

Here $\sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c)$ are the standardised speech and phonological-memory confounders (SP = `deapp_c`, RW = `erbto`), each with a missing-indicator; measured skill parents are excluded as post-treatment mediators. No `kappa` under the Bernoulli. Priors: shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
